commit.message:
Add Marigold SDS depth loss to refine_frame0.py

Replaces the fixed affine-target depth loss with a per-step SDS
(Score Distillation Sampling) loss using Marigold as a depth prior.
The composite depth (bg fixed + fg via depth-as-color) is encoded
through Marigold's VAE; one U-Net denoising step produces a score
gradient that pushes the fg placement toward a realistic depth
consistent with the reference image. Affine mode is retained as
--depth_mode affine for comparison.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: reset isConnected flag in disconnectWebSocket

The isConnected flag was not reset when disconnecting, causing
reconnect logic to fail silently. This prevented WebSocket
subscriptions from being re-established after session resume,
resulting in lost messages.

Fix FunnelChart overflow: use max value instead of first step value

The funnel chart used steps[0].value as the denominator, causing bars to
exceed 100% width when later steps had larger values (e.g. 280K/45K = 622%).
Now uses Math.max() across all steps and caps at 100%. Also reordered the
fan lifecycle funnel data to proper descending order.

commit.message:
motivate mixed-radix codes with restriction site avoidance example

Explains why a DNA storage encoder may need variable radices:
context-dependent constraints (homopolymers, restriction sites, etc.)
can reduce available bases at some positions to 2 or even 1,
requiring the code to adapt its radix per position.

commit.message:
Fix Phase 1 bugs: crawler driver, issue format, domain normalization

BUG 2 (Critical): Crawler was stuck at 0 pages because nothing called
/api/crawl/full/next after starting a job. Added a crawl driver loop
in AuditDomainClient that drives the crawl page-by-page until done.
Uses a ref guard to prevent multiple parallel drivers.

BUG 1: Domain showed 'undefined' when user entered www.example.com —
ProjectsClient redirect didn't strip www before routing, but DB stored
normalized domain. Fixed normalization to match /api/crawl/full/start.

Issue format fix: /api/crawl/full/next was storing issues in the
action-center format {id, priority, title} but /api/projects and
/api/audits/data expected compact {id, sev, msg}. Replaced with
auditPageCompact() that generates ISSUE_META-compatible underscore IDs.

Visibility fields: Added current_url, last_heartbeat_at, last_error to
crawl_jobs (migration 20260305140000). Crawler writes them in real-time
so the UI can show which URL is being fetched and last error.

/api/projects: Support both sev (new) and priority (legacy) formats for
severity counting. Filter out null-domain rows. Return currentUrl and
lastError so the audit page banner shows crawler status.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: clickstream tracking — event card interactions stored per user/anonymous

DB (analytics_schema.sql):
- Add event_clicks table: flat normalized schema with indexed columns (event_id,
  action, user_id, anon_id, session_id, source) + narrow JSONB extras column for
  variable metadata. B-tree indexes on all hot query paths.
- Add event_click_daily table: pre-aggregated daily rollup (PRIMARY KEY on
  event_id+action+date), lives permanently for long-term trend charts.
- Add rollup_event_clicks_daily() PL/pgSQL function: idempotent nightly aggregation
  using INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE.
- 30-day TTL on raw event_clicks; nightly cron runs rollup first, then cleanup.

Backend (server.js):
- POST /api/events/click: no-auth endpoint, validates action enum, inserts into
  event_clicks. Returns 201 { ok: true }.

Frontend:
- utils/anonId.ts: getAnonId() — UUID in localStorage (key: kf_anon_id), set on
  first load, bridges anon → authenticated journeys.
- services/trackClick.ts: trackClick() — uses sendBeacon (with fetch keepalive
  fallback). Fire-and-forget; never throws or blocks UI.
- EventCard.tsx: getStoredUserId() helper; trackClick wired into handleClick
  (view_detail), handleToggleSave (save/unsave), handleShare (share), and ctaAction
  (cta_click with extras.cta_label = button text).

API_README.md: section 7 — full payload docs, action enum table, extras fields,
  two-tier storage design notes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: tiered Neon PostgreSQL data cache for all external API fetches

Adds a synchronous psycopg2-backed cache layer in front of every upstream
API call in data_provider_hybrid.py, eliminating redundant yfinance/SEC
calls across analysis runs.

TTL tiers (configurable via cache_control table):
  - 90 days : company_profile, financial_statements (SEC + quarterly)
  - 7 days  : earnings_calendar, analyst_data
  - 48h     : institutional_ownership, insider_transactions
  - 24h     : short_interest (FINRA)
  - 15 min  : price_snapshot (valuation_metrics + OHLCV DataFrame)

Key design decisions:
  - psycopg2-binary (sync) keeps data_provider_hybrid.py synchronous
  - pandas DataFrames serialized with {"__df__": "<split-json>"} tag for
    transparent round-trip restoration
  - All cache operations wrapped in try/except — analysis always proceeds
    on cache miss or DB error
  - financial_statements combines filings_raw (SEC) + quarterly_financials
    (yfinance) in one 90-day cache row, fetched in a single miss

New files:
  - research_swarm/data/data_cache_service.py — DataCacheService + singleton
  - db/migrations/20260305000000_data_cache/migration.sql — DDL + seed

Modified:
  - db/schema.prisma — 9 new Prisma models (CacheCompanyProfile, etc.)
  - research_swarm/data/data_provider_hybrid.py — cache integration
  - api/routes/admin.py — GET /admin/cache/stats + POST /admin/cache/purge
  - requirements.txt — psycopg2-binary>=2.9.0

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: Context Graph V2 — Property Graph, AI.GENERATE, World Change detection (#23)

* feat: Context Graph V2 — Property Graph, AI.GENERATE extraction, World Change detection (#21)

- Add context_graph.py module: ContextGraphManager with BizNode extraction
  via AI.GENERATE (full Vertex AI endpoint URL for Gemini 3.x+), Property
  Graph DDL with LABEL/KEY following BQ Studio patterns, GQL reasoning
  chain traversal with TO_JSON returns, and World Change drift detection
- Add client.context_graph() factory method
- Export BizNode, ContextGraphConfig, ContextGraphManager, WorldChangeAlert,
  WorldChangeReport from __init__.py
- Add 30 unit tests for all context graph functionality
- Add context_graph_adcp_demo.ipynb: 12-phase customer-facing demo with
  ADCP multi-agent media buying, trace retrieval, entity extraction,
  Property Graph DDL, GQL visualization cells for BQ Studio, world change
  detection, full evaluation pipeline, multi-trial, and AI insights

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: Address P1-P4 review findings for context graph correctness

P1: Use composite keys to prevent collisions/over-merging
  - BizNode keyed by biz_node_id (span_id:node_type:node_value)
  - Evaluated edges keyed by link_id (span_id:node_value)
  - Cross-links table includes biz_node_id for proper referencing

P2: Parameterize biz_entity via @biz_entity query parameter
  - Eliminates SQL injection / query-break risk from entity values
  - explain_decision() conditionally adds the parameter

P3: Make extraction pipeline idempotent
  - AI.GENERATE now uses MERGE upsert (not CREATE OR REPLACE TABLE)
  - create_cross_links() deletes existing session links before insert
  - Re-running for subset of sessions no longer wipes other data

P4: Fix misleading docstring for detect_world_changes
  - Docs now correctly state that current_state_fn=None returns a safe
    report with entity count (no simulated check)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: Address 4 issue-specific findings for Context Graph (#21)

1. Add output_schema to AI.GENERATE extraction SQL for strict
   structured output per issue Step 2
2. Add GQL-based trace reconstruction via reconstruct_trace_gql()
   and Client.get_session_trace_gql() per issue Step 4
3. Model Persisted Artifacts pillar: artifact_uri on BizNode,
   cross-links table, and Evaluated edge properties in DDL
4. Use _WORLD_CHANGE_CHECK_QUERY with evaluated_at timestamp in
   detect_world_changes() for temporal drift semantics

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: Edge-key collision, stale biz node cleanup, GQL fallback

1. Derive cross-link link_id from biz_node_id (includes node_type)
   to prevent collision when same span has entities with same
   node_value but different node_type
2. Add WHEN NOT MATCHED BY SOURCE ... DELETE to MERGE so stale
   biz nodes for target sessions are removed on re-extraction
3. get_session_trace_gql falls back to flat SQL query when GQL
   returns no edges (sparse/flat traces) instead of raising

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: Merge isolated events in GQL trace, return artifact_uri from read APIs

1. GQL trace reconstruction now merges with flat SQL query to
   capture isolated events (no Caused edges) that would otherwise
   be silently dropped in mixed sessions
2. artifact_uri is now selected and mapped in _read_biz_nodes,
   get_biz_nodes_for_session, and _BIZ_NODES_FOR_SESSION_QUERY
   so callers see the persisted artifact linkage

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: Parent linkage backfill, span ordering, artifact_uri in store, delete error handling

1. Backfill parent_span_id when a span is first seen as parent_
   then later as child_ in GQL rows (prevents wrong tree structure)
2. Sort final spans by (timestamp, span_id) for deterministic
   chronological order
3. Include artifact_uri in store_biz_nodes insert payload
4. Cross-link delete now only suppresses "not found" errors;
   real failures (permissions, etc.) propagate as return False

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: datetime import, legacy endpoint rejection, fail-closed world-change

1. Import datetime/timezone in client.py and use timezone-aware
   epoch fallback for span sorting (fixes F821 runtime crash)
2. _resolve_endpoint() rejects legacy BQ ML model refs
   (project.dataset.model) with clear ValueError
3. detect_world_changes() is now fail-closed: query failures
   return check_failed=True, is_safe_to_approve=False instead
   of silently reporting "safe"

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: Fail-closed on current_state_fn errors + clean lint debt

- Track callback failures in detect_world_changes(); set check_failed=True
  and is_safe_to_approve=False when any current_state_fn call throws
- Remove unused imports (asyncio, json) from context_graph.py
- Remove unused local variable in test_extract_query_has_output_schema
- Remove unused Span import in test_get_session_trace_gql_chronological_order
- Update test_detect_world_changes_fn_exception to expect fail-closed behavior

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: Add Context Graph V2 design document

Comprehensive design doc for the Context Graph V2 demo covering:
- 4-Pillar Property Graph architecture (TechNode + BizNode + Caused + Evaluated)
- AI.GENERATE with output_schema for structured entity extraction
- GQL reasoning chains and trace reconstruction
- World-change detection with fail-closed semantics
- MERGE with DELETE for stale BizNode cleanup
- Data model (agent_events, biz_nodes, cross_links)
- Production query patterns (5 SQL/GQL templates)
- Interactive demo features and deployment
- Evolution from V1 to V2

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(wq-858): add staleness guard to e-session-context.md consumption

If e-session-context.md contains session references >5 sessions old
(1 full BBREA cycle), skip it rather than injecting stale engagement
context from a previous E session. Prevents crashed sessions from
polluting the next E session's platform rotation hints and topic
clusters.

Audited all session-context files: only eContext lacks age checks.
Counter files are inherently current, impact/intel/trace files are
accumulated (not session-specific), and todo-followups already has
a staleness guard (wq-852).

commit.message:
feat(tasks): Add linked entities (containers, receipts, shipments) to tasks

## User Request (Original)
Внутри задачи под блоком прикрепленные файлы сделать блок "Связанные сущности" где можно прикреплять контейнер, отгрузку или приход через выпадающий список с поиском

## Interpretation
Add a "Linked Entities" section to task detail view allowing users to attach VED containers, warehouse receipts, and shipments via a searchable dropdown.

## Actions Taken
- Created task_linked_entities DB table (task_id, entity_type, entity_id, UNIQUE constraint)
- Added CSS styles for linked entities section (matching task-files design)
- Added UI block between files and comments in openTaskDetail() with tabs (Containers/Receipts/Shipments), search, and entity list
- Added JS functions: toggle dropdown, switch tabs, search/filter, add/remove entities, navigate to entity
- Added 3 API endpoints: search entities, add link (POST), delete link (DELETE)
- Modified GET /api/tasks/{id} to return linked_entities with resolved titles

## Files Changed
- `ozon_app.py` - DB table, CSS, UI HTML, JS functions, API endpoints

commit.message:
Slash token usage: 3-layer cache, Haiku, leaner prompt

- Server-side in-memory cache by city+date (4h TTL)
- Client-side localStorage cache (instant reload)
- CDN cache headers (s-maxage=14400)
- Switch from Sonnet to Haiku (~25x cheaper)
- Reduce web searches from 5 to 3
- Trim prompt ~60%, improve imageQuery guidance

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Replace Cramer's rule linear solvers with generic LU factorization

Removes the five hard-coded solver functions (determinant2, solve2,
determinant3, solve3, and the static-if dispatch shell of lin_solve)
and replaces them with a single generic lin_solve!(dim) template using
LU factorization with partial pivoting.

Because dim is a compile-time template parameter, the compiler emits a
fully specialised, inlinable version for each distinct system size with
all loop bounds known at compile time — matching the performance profile
of the old hand-coded solvers while removing the static assert barrier
that prevented use beyond dim=3.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Enhance desktop/laptop button visibility with dramatic hover effects

Significantly improved desktop UX by making buttons more visible and interactive on non-touch devices.

Cart Button (Header):
- Added 2px blue border for definition
- Increased shadow prominence (0 6px 20px)
- Hover: Fills solid blue, icon turns white, scales 1.12x
- Much more noticeable on desktop

Product Modal Close Button:
- Increased from 48px to 52px
- Added 2px border and stronger shadow
- Hover: Fills solid red, icon turns white, scales 1.15x + rotates 90°
- Dramatic color transformation

Image Viewer Close Button:
- Changed from dark to bright white (60px)
- Larger icon (30px)
- Hover: Fills solid red, scales 1.18x + rotates 90°, massive glow
- Most dramatic transformation

Cart Bin Icon:
- Increased from size 22 to 26 with thicker stroke
- Button increased from 40px to 44px (Apple HIG)

All hover effects now have:
- More dramatic scale (1.12-1.18x vs 1.08x)
- Solid color fills instead of tints
- Enhanced shadows and glows
- Better visibility on desktop/laptop screens

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: use database.run() for write operations on Android SQLite

database.query() in @capacitor-community/sqlite only supports SELECT
statements (routes to rawQuery/Cursor). INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE silently
failed, causing books to never persist to SQLite on Android.

Also fix Browse page React error #300 (hooks after conditional return)
by moving seriesBookCount useMemo before the early return.

commit.message:
fix: increase Gradle JVM memory and skip lint to prevent OOM

GitHub Actions ran out of JVM Metaspace with expo-updates + expo-dev-client
lint tasks. Increase heap to 4GB, metaspace to 1GB, skip lintVitalAnalyze.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

chore(deps): batch update postcss, expo-notifications, safe-area-context, unlighthouse (#559)

- postcss 8.5.6 → 8.5.8
- expo-notifications 55.0.10 → 55.0.11
- react-native-safe-area-context 5.6.2 → 5.7.0
- @unlighthouse/cli + @unlighthouse/core 0.17.4 → 0.17.7

Supersedes: #553, #554, #555, #558
Closes: #552 (already at 8.56.1 from #551)

commit.message:
fix: mention Spot Check in stats empty state across all locales (#504)

The stats.noData translation only mentioned Flashcard and ACAAN sessions,
but Spot Check sessions also generate stats. Updated all 7 locales to
include Spot Check, respecting each language's capitalization conventions.

feat(subwhisper): v8.36 — FFmpeg 2 modes (Hardcode / Soft Sub)

- HARDCODE : -c:v libx264 -crf 18 -preset fast (qualité haute, re-encode)
- SOFT SUB : -c copy -c:s mov_text (instantané, sans re-encode, piste séparée)

- setFfmpegMode() / updateFfmpegCmd() refactorisés

commit.message:
Add Sanity CMS integration, redesign themes, and add brand assets

Set up Sanity Studio with schemas for services, testimonials, and company
settings. Add client-side Sanity context, hooks, and query helpers. Redesign
CSS themes and variant styles with updated color system. Refine website pages
(homepage, service pages, contact) and navigation components.

commit.message:
fix: activate agent voice – send initiation packet & wait for agent_response

- On open: send { type: "conversation_initiation_client_data" } per ElevenLabs WS protocol
- Wait for agent_response (greeting) before injecting KB text
- Handle ping/pong keepalive
- Collect audio from type=audio messages (audio_event or audio field)
- End on is_final or conversation_end
- Debug logging for every message type received

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
